Fetch model list from baseUrl dynamically (i.e. from `{baseUrl}/models`) ### Why? It makes hooking Pi to llama.cpp/ollama/LM Studio/vLLM much more seamless ### How? (optional) Happy to propose a PR if this is deemed valuable.","number":3357,"state":"OPEN","title":"Official local LLM provider extension","updatedAt":"2026-06-11T08:23:06Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/3357"},{"body":"## Summary `openai-codex` / `gpt-5.5` sometimes leaves the interactive TUI stuck on `Working...` with no streamed text, no tool call, and no visible error. The only way to recover is pressing Escape, which records an aborted assistant turn. This has happened repeatedly over the last couple of days in normal interactive use. ## Environment - pi: `0.75.5` - Node: `v22.22.1` - Provider/model: `openai-codex` / `gpt-5.5` - Thinking level: `xhigh` - No explicit `transport` setting in user settings - Default tools/extensions enabled ## Observed behavior When the issue occurs: - TUI keeps showing `Working...` for minutes. - No assistant text is streamed. - No tool call is emitted. - Pressing Escape aborts the turn. - The saved session entry for that assistant turn has: ```json { "role": "assistant", "stopReason": "aborted", "content": [], "usage": { "input": 0, "output": 0, "cacheRead": 0, "cacheWrite": 0, "totalTokens": 0 } } ``` I saw this pattern multiple times, including cases where the previous turn had completed normally and the next user message then hung before any provider usage was recorded. This looks different from a long reasoning turn: there is no usage, no partial reasoning/text, and no tool call. ## Expected behavior If the provider/transport stalls before the first event, pi should eventually surface a timeout/transport error or retry in a bounded way, instead of keeping the TUI in `Working...` indefinitely until the user manually aborts. ## Suspected area From the installed `0.75.5` package: - `SettingsManager.getTransport()` returns `"auto"` when no setting is present. - The docs/settings table says the default is `"sse"`, so there may also be a docs/runtime default mismatch. - For `openai-codex-responses`, `transport=auto` attempts WebSocket first. - `retry.provider.timeoutMs` appears to be passed into `streamSimple()`, but the `openai-codex-responses` implementation does not seem to apply it to the Codex fetch/WebSocket wait path in the same way as SDK-based providers. - The WebSocket event loop can wait for the first message/completion without an obvious idle timer. So the likely failure mode is: Codex WebSocket/transport waits before the first event; no assistant event is produced; interactive UI keeps showing `Working...`; Escape finally records an aborted zero-usage turn. ## Suggested fix / mitigation Possible fixes: 1. Add a hard idle timeout for `openai-codex-responses` WebSocket and SSE stream waits, especially before the first event. 2. Ensure `retry.provider.timeoutMs` or `httpIdleTimeoutMs` applies consistently to this provider path. 3. If `auto` is intended as the runtime default, update docs; if `sse` is intended, adjust `SettingsManager.getTransport()`. 4. Optionally show a clearer status/error when a provider turn has produced zero events for a long time. Local workaround I am considering, but have not applied yet: ```json { "transport": "sse", "httpIdleTimeoutMs": 120000 } ``` I can provide more sanitized session metadata if helpful, but I avoided attaching raw session logs because they contain private conversation/tool context. ","number":4945,"state":"OPEN","title":"openai-codex Connection Reliability Issues","updatedAt":"2026-06-16T07:35:03Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/4945"},{"body":"### What happened? [The documentation](https://pi.dev/docs/latest/models#openai-compatibility) states that > For providers with partial OpenAI compatibility, use the compat field. > \ > | Field | Description | > | --- | --- | > | `openRouterRouting` | OpenRouter provider routing preferences.
**This object is sent as-is in the provider field of the OpenRouter API request.** | However, in reality the `provider` field is only sent if `baseUrl` contains `"openrouter.ai"`: https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/blob/ea3465a8e371a12d0167a06b60f93878e3a3df44/packages/ai/src/providers/openai-completions.ts#L613 There are cases, when `baseUrl` could be different from `"openrouter.ai"`, but sending the `provider` field should still be sent: 1) Some LLM providers support many options from the OpenRouter API extensions (including the `provider` object), not just the `ai-sdk/openai-compatible` subset. 2) It's possible that `baseUrl` points to a proxy / gateway of some kind that eventually forwards the requests to `openrouter.ai`. In that case, checking `model.baseUrl.includes("openrouter.ai")` doesn't make sense. ### Steps to reproduce 1) Run a local HTTP server that simply logs all HTTP requests before forwarding them to `openrouter.ai`. 2) Use the following `models.json`: ```json { "providers": { "local-openrouter-proxy": { "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1", "api": "openai-completions", "apiKey": "whatever", "models": [ { "id": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro", "compat": {"openRouterRouting": {"only": ["DeepSeek"], "allow_fallbacks": false}} } ] } } } ``` 3) Observe the fact that `pi` silently ignores the `openRouterRouting` key (no `provider` object is inserted into the requests). ### Expected behavior The `openRouterRouting` key should always insert a `provider` object into the request, no matter what `baseUrl` is. I am temporarily working around this issue with this extension: ```ts import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent"; export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) { pi.on("before_provider_request", (event, ctx) => { const provider = ctx.model?.compat?.openRouterRouting; if (provider !== undefined) { return { ...event.payload, provider }; } }); } ``` But ideally, I think, this should be fixed in `pi` itself. ### Version 0.76.0","number":5347,"state":"CLOSED","title":"`openai-completions` silently ignores `compat.openRouterRouting` if `baseUrl` is not `openrouter.ai`","updatedAt":"2026-06-04T21:49:31Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5347"},{"body":"### What happened? using openai completions providers (like Together.ai) reasoning models (like DeepSeek v4pro) are running out of output tokens before turn finishes, regardless of my settings. ### Steps to reproduce set TOGETHER_API_KEY and run deepseek v4 pro in a brand new install of pi. if it reasons too long it will just stop. ### Expected behavior i should be able to set high enough output tokens to use together.ai reasoning models. In `packages/ai/src/providers/openai-completions.ts`, fall back to `model.maxTokens` when `options.maxTokens` is unset, matching what `anthropic.ts` already does. Current (`openai-completions.ts:526`): ```ts if (options?.maxTokens) { if (compat.maxTokensField === "max_tokens") { (params as any).max_tokens = options.maxTokens; } else { params.max_completion_tokens = options.maxTokens; } } ``` Proposed: ```ts const maxTokens = options?.maxTokens ?? model.maxTokens; if (maxTokens) { if (compat.maxTokensField === "max_tokens") { (params as any).max_tokens = maxTokens; } else { params.max_completion_tokens = maxTokens; } } ``` ### Version 0.79.1","number":5595,"state":"OPEN","title":"openai-completions maxTokens not passing through","updatedAt":"2026-06-13T17:26:44Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5595"},{"body":"### What do you want to change? Serialize system prompts into `instructions` field in `openai-responses` / `azure-openai-responses` rather than current `system` or `developer`. ### Why? Per [OpenAI Developer](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/text#message-roles-and-instruction-following) and [Azure Foundry](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/openai/how-to/responses?tabs=python#code-interpreter), `instructions` is where the system prompt should go. Changing to this will make Responses API usage more "canonical". [AWS Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/bedrock-mantle.html) says it provides the OpenAI Responses API and points to OpenAI's doc, so should be good as well. ### How? (optional) Happy to take this one, but wanna discuss the directions and maybe caveats before landing a PR.","number":5858,"state":"OPEN","title":"align and use "instructions" field for openai-responses system prompt","updatedAt":"2026-06-17T23:42:05Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5858"},{"body":"## What do you want to change? Add a new `amazon-bedrock-mantle` provider to `packages/ai`. The existing `amazon-bedrock` provider uses the Converse API; Bedrock Mantle models use an OpenAI-compatible API at `bedrock-mantle.{region}.api.aws/openai/v1/responses` and are incompatible with Converse. As noted in #5338, a separate provider is required. ## Why? AWS is rolling out a class of models on the Bedrock Mantle endpoint that cannot be used through the existing `amazon-bedrock` provider. Without this provider, those models are permanently inaccessible in pi. ## How? - New provider `amazon-bedrock-mantle` in `packages/ai`, wired to the OpenAI-compatible Bedrock Mantle endpoint - Auth via AWS credential chain (SigV4), targeting `bedrock-mantle.{region}.api.aws` - `openai.gpt-5.4` and `openai.gpt-5.5` hard-coded in the model catalog for now; `models.dev` does not yet track these models (see [anomalyco/models.dev#1962](https://github.com/anomalyco/models.dev/issues/1962))","number":5363,"state":"OPEN","title":"Add amazon-bedrock-mantle provider for OpenAI-compatible models","updatedAt":"2026-06-15T13:23:07Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5363"},{"body":"### What happened? Behind a proxy/gateway, when an endpoint returns a non-2xx with a body the SDK can't parse into its schema, most providers drop the body. The same 403 surfaces differently per provider: - Bedrock → `Unknown: UnknownError` - OpenAI completions → `403 status code (no body)` - Gemini / Vertex / Codex Responses → the SDK's opaque message - OpenAI / Azure Responses → status shown, body dropped - Anthropic, Mistral → the real reason (correct) Each provider builds `output.errorMessage` from `error.message` (some add a status code), never falling back to the raw body the SDK still carries. `formatMistralError` (`packages/ai/src/providers/mistral.ts`) already reads `sdkError.statusCode` + `sdkError.body` — that's the pattern everyone should use. Affected files: `amazon-bedrock.ts`, `openai-completions.ts`, `google.ts`, `google-vertex.ts`, `openai-codex-responses.ts` (body-blind); `openai-responses.ts`, `azure-openai-responses.ts` (status-only). Anthropic works only because its SDK folds the body into `error.message`. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Put any OpenAI-compatible / Bedrock-Converse / Gemini proxy in front that returns `403` with `{"error":""}`. 2. Point pi at it and send a request it rejects. 3. The surfaced error is opaque (`Unknown: UnknownError`, `403 status code (no body)`, etc.) instead of the reason in the body. ### Expected behavior When the SDK parses the body into `error.message`, keep showing that. Otherwise fall back to HTTP status + raw body — e.g. `403: {"error":""}` — the way Mistral already does. Cleanest as one shared helper all `catch` blocks route through. I'm happy to implement this; no new dependency (the fields already exist on the SDK error objects). Happy to implement the fix myself if you lgtm. ### Version 0.79.1","number":5763,"state":"OPEN","title":"Providers swallow the HTTP error body, so gateway / non-schema errors are unreadable","updatedAt":"2026-06-17T07:30:11Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5763"},{"body":"### What happened? options.maxTokens in pi-ai is silently ignored for opencode-go provider. The API accepts max_tokens but pi-ai sends max_completion_tokens, which the backend ignores. ### Steps to reproduce Send streamSimple(model, ctx, { maxTokens: 1 }) for any opencode-go model -- output is not truncated. ### Expected behavior maxTokens maps to "max_tokens", not "max_completion_tokens" ### Version 0.78.0","number":5331,"state":"CLOSED","title":"options.maxTokens in pi-ai maps to wrong API parameter for opencode-go provider","updatedAt":"2026-06-09T11:41:23Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5331"},{"body":"### What happened? Component: @earendil-works/pi-ai — providers/openai-completions.js and providers/openai-completions.ts ### Description When using an OpenAI-compatible provider (LM Studio, Ollama, vLLM, local llama.cpp, etc.), the maxTokens value set in models.json is silently ignored. The buildParams() function only sends max_tokens/max_completion_tokens when options?.maxTokens is truthy, but the agent loop never sets options.maxTokens — it only passes the model object reference. The Anthropic provider has the correct fallback: options?.maxTokens ?? model.maxTokens. Impact: The API receives no output token limit, so the server applies its default (often 512–2048 for llama.cpp-based servers). Long tool calls (e.g., write with >5k tokens of content) get truncated mid-JSON, causing parse failures and incomplete writes. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ### Root Cause Two issues in openai-completions.js: 1. Missing model.maxTokens fallback in buildParams() (~line 401): ```js // ❌ Current: only checks options.maxTokens, no fallback to model.maxTokens if (options?.maxTokens) { if (compat.maxTokensField === "max_tokens") { params.max_tokens = options.maxTokens; } else { params.max_completion_tokens = options.maxTokens; } } ``` Compare with the Anthropic provider (anthropic.js:664): ```js // ✅ Has fallback max_tokens: options?.maxTokens ?? model.maxTokens, ``` 2. No detection of local/private servers in detectCompat() (~line 895). The useMaxTokens boolean only triggers for known SaaS providers: ```js const useMaxTokens = baseUrl.includes("chutes.ai") || isMoonshot || isCloudflareAiGateway || isTogether; ``` Local servers (LM Studio, Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM) use llama.cpp's OpenAI-compatible API which only understands max_tokens, not max_completion_tokens. Without detection, they get the wrong field name (max_completion_tokens instead of max_tokens). Data flow trace: ``` models.json → model.maxTokens = 65572 ✅ stored correctly agent.js (createLoopConfig) → does not include maxTokens in config ⚠️ agent-loop.js (streamAssistantResponse) → ...config → options.maxTokens = undefined ⚠️ openai-completions.js (buildParams) → if (options?.maxTokens) → SKIPPED ❌ ``` ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ### Fix In openai-completions.js, buildParams(): Replace: ```js if (options?.maxTokens) { ``` with: ```js const effectiveMaxTokens = options?.maxTokens ?? model.maxTokens; if (effectiveMaxTokens) { ``` In openai-completions.js, detectCompat(): Add local server detection to useMaxTokens: ```js const isLocalServer = baseUrl.includes("localhost") || baseUrl.includes("127.0.0.1") || baseUrl.includes("10.") || baseUrl.includes("192.168."); const useMaxTokens = ...existing... || isLocalServer; ``` Or, alternatively, make the maxTokensField detection broader — any server that isn't a known OpenAI/OpenAI-compatible SaaS endpoint should default to max_tokens since that's the universally supported field name. For the TypeScript source (openai-completions.ts), the same changes apply. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ### Workaround Users can work around issue #2 (wrong field name) by setting explicit compat on their provider: ```json "compat": { "maxTokensField": "max_tokens" } ``` But issue #1 (missing fallback) cannot be worked around without patching the source. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ### Environment - pi version: @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent latest (as of June 2026) - Model: LM Studio with google/gemma-4-e4b, contextWindow/maxTokens configured at 65572 - API type: openai-completions ### Steps to reproduce 1. Configure a local LM Studio provider in ~/.pi/agent/models.json: ```json { "providers": { "lmstudio": { "baseUrl": "http://localhost:1234/v1", "api": "openai-completions", "apiKey": "lm-studio", "models": [ { "id": "my-model", "contextWindow": 65572, "maxTokens": 65572 } ] } } } ``` 2. Set the model in pi (/model). Set thinking to off. 3. Ask it to write a ~10,000 character file. 4. Expected: File is written successfully. 5. Actual: The write tool call is truncated mid-JSON. Server returns finish_reason: "length" with incomplete content. Verified with LM Studio running google/gemma-4-e4b. ### Expected behavior The maxTokens value configured in models.json should be sent to the OpenAI-compatible API as either max_tokens or max_completion_tokens (depending on the provider's compatibility). The model should be able to generate up to the configured number of output tokens. Concretely, for the example above: - Given "maxTokens": 65572 in the model config - When pi sends the API request to LM Studio - Then the request should include "max_tokens": 65572 - And a 10,000-character write should complete without truncation ### Version _No response_","number":5375,"state":"CLOSED","title":"OpenAI Completions provider ignores model.maxTokens — no fallback when options.maxTokens is undefined","updatedAt":"2026-06-03T21:22:29Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5375"},{"body":"### Summary `@earendil-works/pi-ai` already supports OpenAI's `service_tier` request parameter (priority/flex) on the Responses API path, including tier-based cost multipliers — but the coding agent never sets `options.serviceTier` and exposes no config sur…293 tokens truncated… per-model `serviceTier?: "auto" | "default" | "flex" | "priority" | "scale"` field in `modelOverrides` / `ProviderModelConfig` (and/or a provider-level default, and/or a runtime toggle), so the existing pi-ai capability — including its tier-aware cost accounting — becomes reachable for `openai-responses` / `openai-codex-responses` models. ### Version `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` 0.79.6 (bundled `@earendil-works/pi-ai` 0.79.6). ","number":5840,"state":"CLOSED","title":"Expose OpenAI service_tier (priority/flex) from coding-agent config","updatedAt":"2026-06-17T12:59:58Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5840"},{"body":"### What happened? `pi.registerProvider()` ignores `models[].baseUrl` and always applies the provider-level `baseUrl` to all registered models. This is inconsistent with: - the exposed type (`ProviderConfigInput.models[].baseUrl` exists), and - `models.json` behavior (which supports per-model `baseUrl`). In `packages/coding-agent/src/core/model-registry.ts`, `applyProviderConfig()` currently sets: `baseUrl: config.baseUrl!` for every model, instead of using `modelDef.baseUrl` when provided. I am working on a plugin for axonhub which is a model hub providing different models in different base url. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create an extension that calls: ```ts pi.registerProvider("my-provider", { baseUrl: "https://default.example/v1", apiKey: "MY_PROVIDER_API_KEY", api: "openai-completions", models: [ { id: "model-a", name: "Model A", baseUrl: "https://a.example/v1", reasoning: false, input: ["text"], cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }, contextWindow: 128000, maxTokens: 4096, }, { id: "model-b", name: "Model B", baseUrl: "https://b.example/v1", reasoning: false, input: ["text"], cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }, contextWindow: 128000, maxTokens: 4096, }, ], }); ``` 2. Start pi and inspect the registered models in the registry/runtime. 3. Check baseUrl for model-a and model-b. ### Expected behavior ### Expected behavior - model-a.baseUrl should be https://a.example/v1 - model-b.baseUrl should be https://b.example/v1 Per-model baseUrl should take precedence over provider-level baseUrl in registerProvider, matching models.json semantics. ### Version 0.71.0","number":4063,"state":"CLOSED","title":"plugin: registerProvider ignores models[].baseUrl and always uses provider-level baseUrl","updatedAt":"2026-05-01T20:18:55Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/4063"},{"body":"### What happened? When attempting to route traffic through a custom OpenAI-compatible proxy (such as Ultimate AI) by overriding the baseUrl of the native "openai" provider in models.json, the agent hard-crashes immediately upon sending the first prompt. The native handler fails to parse the proxy's stream correctly, resulting in an unhandled .push error on the session's summary object. ### Steps to reproduce Ensure you are running Pi version 0.76.0. Configure ~/.pi/agent/models.json to override the built-in openai provider: JSON { "providers": { "openai": { "baseUrl": "https://chat.ultimateai.org/v1" } } } Authenticate the provider (either via auth.json or by injecting the API key). Launch the agent in the terminal using pi. Send a simple prompt (e.g., hi). The agent instantly crashes. ### Expected behavior Expected Behavior The agent should gracefully handle slight variances in the stream structure from custom OpenAI-compatible proxies. If the stream lacks the expected formatting for session compaction/summaries, Pi should fall back safely or throw a descriptive validation error rather than crashing the entire process. Actual Behavior The agent crashes with the following error output: Plaintext Error: currentItem.summary.push is not a function ### Version 0.76.0","number":5126,"state":"CLOSED","title":"bug: Error: currentItem.summary.push is not a function when overriding native openai provider baseUrl","updatedAt":"2026-05-28T11:44:16Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5126"},{"body":"### What happened? When attempting to route traffic through a custom OpenAI-compatible proxy (such as Ultimate AI) by overriding the baseUrl of the native "openai" provider in models.json, the agent hard-crashes immediately upon sending the first prompt. The native handler fails to parse the proxy's stream correctly, resulting in an unhandled .push error on the session's summary object. ### Steps to reproduce Ensure you are running Pi version 0.76.0. Configure ~/.pi/agent/models.json to override the built-in openai provider: JSON { "providers": { "openai": { "baseUrl": "https://chat.ultimateai.org/v1" } } } Authenticate the provider (either via auth.json or by injecting the API key using /login) Launch the agent in the terminal using pi. Send a simple prompt (e.g., hi). The agent instantly crashes. ### Expected behavior The agent should gracefully handle slight variances in the stream structure from custom OpenAI-compatible proxies. If the stream lacks the expected formatting for session compaction/summaries, Pi should fall back safely or throw a descriptive validation error rather than crashing the entire process. Actual Behavior The agent crashes with the following error output: Plaintext Error: currentItem.summary.push is not a function Additional Context / Troubleshooting Done The issue is not caused by stale or corrupted local session cache. Running rm -rf ~/.pi/agent/sessions/ and launching with --session-id fresh-start results in the exact same crash. ### Version v0.76.0 and v0.75.5","number":5125,"state":"CLOSED","title":"bug: Error: currentItem.summary.push is not a function when overriding native openai provider baseUrl","updatedAt":"2026-05-28T11:41:29Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5125"},{"body":"### What happened? During `/login litellm`, the login dialog prompts for the LiteLLM base URL and then for the API key. When typing into the API-key prompt, the same text is also rendered in the earlier base-URL prompt row. The underlying values are captured sequentially, but the UI is misleading and visually leaks the active prompt value into a previous prompt slot. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Run `/login litellm`. 2. Enter a LiteLLM base URL and submit it. 3. Start typing the API key in the next prompt. 4. Observe that the API key text appears in both the API-key row and the earlier base-URL prompt row. ### Expected behavior Only the active prompt should render a live input. Previous prompts should remain visible as stable submitted text, or otherwise not render the active input value again. OAuth flows that call `showAuth()` before `showPrompt()` should still keep the auth URL and instructions visible. ### Version 0.76.0 / current main. ### Notes The likely cause is that `LoginDialogComponent` creates one `Input` instance and `showPrompt()` appends that same live input each time. Commit `9c87eeb8` intentionally stopped clearing the content container so auth URLs remain visible, so a fix should avoid clearing the whole dialog content. ","number":5292,"state":"CLOSED","title":"Login dialog mirrors active OAuth prompt input into previous rows","updatedAt":"2026-06-02T13:14:47Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5292"},{"body":"### What do you want to change? Add a small built-in `qianfan` provider that uses the existing `openai-completions` implementation with Baidu Qianfan's OpenAI-compatible v2 endpoint: - provider: `qianfan` - base URL: `https://qianfan.baidubce.com/v2` - env var: `QIANFAN_API_KEY` - auth key: `qianfan` - initial model list: a small ERNIE 4.5 set with tool-capable models only, based on Qianfan's public docs This would also add the provider to `/login`, model resolution, provider docs, and the existing provider E2E matrix guarded by `QIANFAN_API_KEY`. ### Why? Pi currently exposes Baidu ERNIE models indirectly through OpenRouter model IDs, but users with a Baidu Qianfan account cannot select Qianfan as a first-class provider. Qianfan's v2 API is OpenAI SDK-compatible, so this should not require a new streaming implementation or a Baidu SDK dependency. It can reuse the same code path as other OpenAI-compatible providers. ### How? (optional) The minimal implementation would: - add `qianfan` to `KnownProvider` - add `QIANFAN_API_KEY` lookup - register display/default model metadata - add Qianfan model metadata with `api: "openai-completions"` and `baseUrl: "https://qianfan.baidubce.com/v2"` - set explicit OpenAI-compat flags only where Qianfan needs them - add docs and `QIANFAN_API_KEY`-gated provider tests Before opening a PR, I would validate this with a real `QIANFAN_API_KEY` against Qianfan's v2 endpoint. The smoke test would cover basic text generation, streaming, and tool calling for the initial ERNIE model set, and only include thinking/multimodal claims if those paths are verified with the selected models. I would keep this limited to Qianfan/ERNIE chat completions and leave Baidu AI Search, Paddle, and other Baidu services out of scope. ","number":4997,"state":"CLOSED","title":"Add built-in Baidu Qianfan provider for ERNIE models","updatedAt":"2026-05-26T03:10:32Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/4997"},{"body":"### Bug Description When registering a custom provider via `pi.registerProvider()` in an extension, the **provider-level** `compat` object is silently ignored for each model. Only the **model-level** `compat` is used. This causes a 400 error (`developer is not one of ['system', 'assistant', 'user', 'tool', 'function']`) when the target API does not support the `developer` role, even though `supportsDeveloperRole: false` is correctly set at the provider level. ### Reproduction 1. Create an extension that registers a custom OpenAI-compatible provider with provider-level compat: ```typescript const SHARED_COMPAT = { supportsDeveloperRole: false, maxTokensField: "max_tokens" as const, }; pi.registerProvider("my-provider", { name: "My Provider", baseUrl: "https://api.example.com/v1", apiKey: "$MY_API_KEY", api: "openai-completions", compat: SHARED_COMPAT, models: [ { id: "my-model", name: "My Model", reasoning: true, input: ["text"], contextWindow: 128000, maxTokens: 4096, cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }, }, ], }); ``` 2. Start pi and send any message. 3. **Expected:** The system prompt is sent with `role: "system"` (per `supportsDeveloperRole: false`). 4. **Actual:** The system prompt is sent with `role: "developer"`, causing a 400 error from the API. ### Root Cause Analysis In `dist/core/model-registry.js`, there are two code paths that resolve a model's `compat`: **Path 1 — `models.json` loading (correct):** ```javascript // ~line 495 const compat = mergeCompat(providerConfig.compat, modelDef.compat); ``` This correctly merges provider-level compat into each model. **Path 2 — `registerProvider()` runtime (buggy):** ```javascript // ~line 760, inside applyProviderConfig() this.models.push({ id: modelDef.id, name: modelDef.name, api: api, provider: providerName, baseUrl: modelDef.baseUrl ?? config.baseUrl, reasoning: modelDef.reasoning, thinkingLevelMap: modelDef.thinkingLevelMap, input: modelDef.input, cost: modelDef.cost, contextWindow: modelDef.contextWindow, maxTokens: modelDef.maxTokens, headers: undefined, compat: modelDef.compat, // ← BUG: provider-level config.compat is not merged }); ``` Only `modelDef.compat` is used; `config.compat` (the provider-level compat) is completely ignored. ### Workaround Explicitly set `compat` on each model definition: ```typescript models: [ { id: "my-model", // ... compat: { ...SHARED_COMPAT }, // duplicate per model }, ], ``` This works but is verbose and error-prone when registering many models with shared compat settings. ### Suggested Fix In `applyProviderConfig()`, merge provider-level compat with model-level compat when pushing models: ```javascript compat: mergeCompat(config.compat, modelDef.compat), ``` This would make the `registerProvider()` path consistent with the `models.json` loading path. ### Environment - pi version: latest (via npm) - OS: Linux - API type: `openai-completions` with a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint ","number":5349,"state":"CLOSED","title":"registerProvider() ignores provider-level compat, causing developer role errors","updatedAt":"2026-06-04T21:44:11Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5349"},{"body":"### What do you want to change? The `google-vertex` adapter doesn't forward `model.baseUrl` into the `@google/genai` SDK, so there's no way to point Vertex models at a proxy or gateway. Every other adapter (`openai-completions`, `anthropic`, `google-gemini-cli`, etc.) honors `model.baseUrl`; Vertex is the odd one out. Concretely, `packages/ai/src/providers/google-vertex.ts`'s `createClient` / `createClientWithApiKey` only plumb `model.headers` into `httpOptions`; they never read `model.baseUrl`. The underlying SDK supports it — `HttpOptions.baseUrl` and `baseUrlResourceScope` both exist on `@google/genai` — but the adapter doesn't set them. ### Why? Pointing pi-mono's `google-vertex` adapter at that gateway requires the SDK to hit `{gatewayUrl}/publishers/google/models/{model}:streamGenerateContent` instead of `{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/...`. With only headers plumbed through, the URL is unreachable from outside Google's infra. Three previous issues describe exactly this use case and were auto-closed before any review: - #2495 - #2556 - #2821 ### How? (optional) In both `createClient` and `createClientWithApiKey`, read `model.baseUrl` and, when present, set: ```js httpOptions.baseUrl = model.baseUrl; httpOptions.baseUrlResourceScope = "COLLECTION"; ``` `COLLECTION` scope stops the SDK from prepending `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/` — the proxy is expected to own project/location context. The existing `headers` merge stays as-is.","number":3619,"state":"CLOSED","title":"`google-vertex` forward model.baseUrl to @google/genai httpOptions","updatedAt":"2026-05-07T16:56:04Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/3619"},{"body":"### What do you want to change? Extend the `prompt` RPC command to forward video and audio content to the LLM, alongside the existing `images` support. Currently `prompt` accepts `images: [{ type: "image", mimeType, data }]`. I'd like the same for video/audio so multimodal models (Gemma 4, GPT-4o) can process them. ### Why? We embed Pi in [screenpipe](https://github.com/screenpipe/screenpipe) as the chat backend. Users want to ask questions about their screen recordings ("what was I doing in this clip?"). We have Gemma 4 running on a GPU that accepts `video_url`/`audio_url` content parts via the OpenAI API — Pi just needs to pass them through. ### How? (optional) Simplest: allow non-image mimeTypes in the existing `images` array and map them to the correct OpenAI content part type (`video_url` for `video/*`, `audio_url` for `audio/*`). No new fields needed.","number":3200,"state":"OPEN","title":"Support video/audio content in prompt command","updatedAt":"2026-06-17T08:57:16Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/3200"},{"body":"## What do you want to change? Add a new `thinkingFormat` value (e.g. `"vllm-deepseek"`) that sends `chat_template_kwargs: { thinking: true }` when reasoning is enabled. This is the format vLLM expects for DeepSeek-V3.x models — the Jinja2 chat template in the model's `tokenizer_config.json` uses a boolean `thinking` variable, not `enable_thinking` (Qwen3) or `{ type: "enabled" }` (native DeepSeek API). ## Why? We run DeepSeek models locally in a corporate environment behind a LiteLLM proxy that talks to vLLM. pi's existing `"deepseek"` thinking format sends `thinking: { type: "enabled" }`, which works against the native DeepSeek API but not against vLLM. The `"qwen-chat-template"` format sends `enable_thinking` — also wrong kwarg name for DeepSeek-V3.x. Currently we work around this with a `before_provider_request` extension that rewrites the payload: ```ts import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent"; export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) { pi.on("before_provider_request", (event, ctx) => { try { const model = ctx.model; if (!model || model.provider !== "mycompany") return; const payload = event.payload as Record; const thinking = payload.thinking as { type?: string } | undefined; const chatTemplateKwargs = payload.chat_template_kwargs as | Record | undefined; const thinkingEnabled = thinking?.type === "enabled" || !!payload.reasoning_effort || !!chatTemplateKwargs?.enable_thinking; if (thinkingEnabled) { payload.chat_template_kwargs = { thinking: true }; } delete payload.thinking; } catch { // silently ignore } }); } ``` I understand this may be too niche — "DeepSeek behind vLLM via a custom provider" is a narrow configuration. But `chat_template_kwargs` is a standard vLLM mechanism and as self-hosted models grow, this pattern may become more common. Would appreciate maintainers' take on whether this belongs in core. ","number":5673,"state":"OPEN","title":"Add "vllm-deepseek" thinking format for DeepSeek models behind vLLM proxies","updatedAt":"2026-06-12T21:52:20Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/5673"},{"body":"## What happened? Prompt caching does not work when using the `openai-responses` API path with non-OpenAI providers (proxies like theclawbay, litellm, etc.). The server-side cache routing headers `session_id` and `x-client-request-id` are never sent, so every request is a cache miss. Three issues in `packages/ai/src/providers/`: 1. **openai-responses.ts:181** — `session_id` and `x-client-request-id` headers are gated behind `model.provider === "openai" && model.baseUrl.includes("api.openai.com")`. The official Codex CLI (`codex-rs`) sends these unconditionally. 2. **openai-responses-shared.ts:487** — `cacheWrite` is hardcoded to `0`. The response's `input_tokens_details.cache_write_tokens` is never read. 3. **openai-codex-responses.ts:910** — `extractAccountId()` throws on non-JWT tokens, blocking third-party providers from using the codex-responses path entirely. ## Steps to reproduce 1. Configure a provider with `api: "openai-responses"` pointing at a non-OpenAI base URL 2. Send multiple requests in the same session 3. Observe `cached_tokens: 0` on every request (confirmed via provider dashboard) ## Expected behavior Cache headers should be sent regardless of provider, matching the official Codex CLI behavior. Second+ requests should show cache hits. **Version:** 0.67.2 I have a working fix and would like to submit a PR.","number":3196,"state":"CLOSED","title":"Prompt caching broken for OpenAI-compatible proxies (openai-responses path)","updatedAt":"2026-04-27T13:11:07Z","url":"https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/3196"}]

payload.message:
Yes, but not as an open exact issue.

The closest upstream issue is [earendil-works/pi#8: “Add support for setting base URLs”](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/8). It specifically asks for `OPENAI_BASE_URL=<custom_openai_base_url>` and a custom OpenAI base URL. It is closed. The maintainer resolved it by supporting custom providers/model config, not by adding `OPENAI_BASE_URL` or `--base-url` to the built-in `openai` CLI provider. ([github.com](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/8?utm_source=openai))

I did not find an open issue specifically saying: “`pi --provider openai` should support `OPENAI_BASE_URL` / `--base-url`.”

Related but not exact:

- [#4561](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/4561) added/requested LiteLLM as a first-class OpenAI-compatible provider with `LITELLM_BASE_URL`. ([github.com](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/4561?utm_source=openai))
- [#3196](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/3196) covers behavior for OpenAI-compatible proxies on the `openai-responses` path, but not CLI base URL selection. ([github.com](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/3196?utm_source=openai))

So if we want clean `pi-cli` support for WTG’s OpenAI-compatible endpoint via `subprovider: openai`, we should raise a new narrow upstream issue: add `--base-url` and/or `OPENAI_BASE_URL` support for the built-in OpenAI provider in `pi` CLI.

Yes, but not as an open exact issue.

The closest upstream issue is [earendil-works/pi#8: “Add support for setting base URLs”](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/8). It specifically asks for `OPENAI_BASE_URL=<custom_openai_base_url>` and a custom OpenAI base URL. It is closed. The maintainer resolved it by supporting custom providers/model config, not by adding `OPENAI_BASE_URL` or `--base-url` to the built-in `openai` CLI provider. ([github.com](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/8?utm_source=openai))

I did not find an open issue specifically saying: “`pi --provider openai` should support `OPENAI_BASE_URL` / `--base-url`.”

Related but not exact:

- [#4561](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/4561) added/requested LiteLLM as a first-class OpenAI-compatible provider with `LITELLM_BASE_URL`. ([github.com](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi/issues/4561?utm_source=openai))

So if we want clean `pi-cli` support for WTG’s OpenAI-compatible endpoint via `subprovider: openai`, we should raise a new narrow upstream issue: add `--base-url` and/or `OPENAI_BASE_URL` support for the built-in OpenAI provider in `pi` CLI.

   128	| `google-generative-ai` | Google Generative AI |
   129	
   130	Set `api` at provider level (default for all models) or model level (override per model).
   131	
   132	## Provider Configuration
   133	
   134	| Field | Description |
   135	|-------|-------------|
   136	| `baseUrl` | API endpoint URL |
   137	| `api` | API type (see above) |
   138	| `apiKey` | API key (see value resolution below) |
   139	| `headers` | Custom headers (see value resolution below) |
   140	| `authHeader` | Set `true` to add `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>` automatically |
   141	| `models` | Array of model configurations |
   142	| `modelOverrides` | Per-model overrides for built-in models on this provider |
   143	
   144	### Value Resolution
   145	
   146	The `apiKey` and `headers` fields support command execution, environment interpolation, and literals:
   147	
   148	- **Shell command:** `"!command"` at the start executes the whole value as a command and uses stdout
   149	  ```json
   150	  "apiKey": "!security find-generic-password -ws 'anthropic'"
   151	  "apiKey": "!op read 'op://vault/item/credential'"
   152	  ```
   153	- **Environment interpolation:** `"$ENV_VAR"` or `"${ENV_VAR}"` uses the value of the named variable. Interpolation works inside larger literals.
   154	  ```json
   155	  "apiKey": "$MY_API_KEY"
   156	  "apiKey": "${KEY_PREFIX}_${KEY_SUFFIX}"
   157	  ```
   158	  `$FOO_BAR` is the variable `FOO_BAR`; use `${FOO}_BAR` when `BAR` is literal text. Missing environment variables make the value unresolved.
   159	- **Escapes:** `"$$"` emits a literal `"$"`; `"$!"` emits a literal `"!"` without triggering command execution.
   160	  ```json
   161	  "apiKey": "$$literal-dollar-prefix"
   162	  "apiKey": "$!literal-bang-prefix"
   163	  ```
   164	- **Literal value:** Used directly. Plain uppercase strings such as `MY_API_KEY` are literals; use `$MY_API_KEY` for environment variables.
   165	  ```json
   166	  "apiKey": "sk-..."
   167	  ```
   168	
   169	For `models.json`, shell commands are resolved at request time. pi intentionally does not apply built-in TTL, stale reuse, or recovery logic for arbitrary commands. Different commands need different caching and failure strategies, and pi cannot infer the right one.
   170	
   171	If your command is slow, expensive, rate-limited, or should keep using a previous value on transient failures, wrap it in your own script or command that implements the caching or TTL behavior you want.
   172	
   173	`/model` availability checks use configured auth presence and do not execute shell commands.
   174	
   175	### Custom Headers
   176	
   177	```json
   178	{
   179	  "providers": {
   180	    "custom-proxy": {
   181	      "baseUrl": "https://proxy.example.com/v1",
   182	      "apiKey": "$MY_API_KEY",
   183	      "api": "anthropic-messages",
   184	      "headers": {
   185	        "x-portkey-api-key": "$PORTKEY_API_KEY",
   186	        "x-secret": "!op read 'op://vault/item/secret'"
   187	      },
   188	      "models": [...]
   189	    }
   190	  }
   191	}
   192	```
   193	
   194	## Model Configuration
   195	
   196	| Field | Required | Default | Description |
   197	|-------|----------|---------|-------------|
   198	| `id` | Yes | — | Model identifier (passed to the API) |
   199	| `name` | No | `id` | Human-readable model label. Used for matching (`--model` patterns) and shown as secondary model detail text. |
   200	| `api` | No | provider's `api` | Override provider's API for this model |
   201	| `reasoning` | No | `false` | Supports extended thinking |
   202	| `thinkingLevelMap` | No | omitted | Maps pi thinking levels to provider values and marks unsupported levels (see below) |
   203	| `input` | No | `["text"]` | Input types: `["text"]` or `["text", "image"]` |
   204	| `contextWindow` | No | `128000` | Context window size in tokens |
   205	| `maxTokens` | No | `16384` | Maximum output tokens |
   206	| `cost` | No | all zeros | `{"input": 0, "output": 0, "cacheRead": 0, "cacheWrite": 0}` (per million tokens) |
   207	| `compat` | No | provider `compat` | Provider compatibility overrides. Merged with provider-level `compat` when both are set. |
   208	
   209	Current behavior:
   210	- `/model`, `--list-models`, and the interactive footer display entries by model `id`.
    30	
    31	## Quick Reference
    32	
    33	```typescript
    34	import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
    35	
    36	export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) {
    37	  // Override baseUrl for existing provider
    38	  pi.registerProvider("anthropic", {
    39	    baseUrl: "https://proxy.example.com"
    40	  });
    41	
    42	  // Register new provider with models
    43	  pi.registerProvider("my-provider", {
    44	    name: "My Provider",
    45	    baseUrl: "https://api.example.com",
    46	    apiKey: "$MY_API_KEY",
    47	    api: "openai-completions",
    48	    models: [
    49	      {
    50	        id: "my-model",
    51	        name: "My Model",
    52	        reasoning: false,
    53	        input: ["text", "image"],
    54	        cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 },
    55	        contextWindow: 128000,
    56	        maxTokens: 4096
    57	      }
    58	    ]
    59	  });
    60	}
    61	```
    62	
    63	The extension factory can also be `async`. For dynamic model discovery, fetch and register models in the factory instead of `session_start`. pi waits for the factory before startup continues, so the provider is available during interactive startup and to `pi --list-models`.
    64	
    65	## Override Existing Provider
    66	
    67	The simplest use case: redirect an existing provider through a proxy.
    68	
    69	```typescript
    70	// All Anthropic requests now go through your proxy
    71	pi.registerProvider("anthropic", {
    72	  baseUrl: "https://proxy.example.com"
    73	});
    74	
    75	// Add custom headers to OpenAI requests
    76	pi.registerProvider("openai", {
    77	  headers: {
    78	    "X-Custom-Header": "value"
    79	  }
    80	});
    81	
    82	// Both baseUrl and headers
    83	pi.registerProvider("google", {
    84	  baseUrl: "https://ai-gateway.corp.com/google",
    85	  headers: {
    86	    "X-Corp-Auth": "$CORP_AUTH_TOKEN"  // env var or literal
    87	  }
    88	});
    89	```
    90	
    91	When only `baseUrl` and/or `headers` are provided (no `models`), all existing models for that provider are preserved with the new endpoint.
    92	
    93	## Register New Provider
    94	
    95	To add a completely new provider, specify `models` along with the required configuration.

payload.message: The Pi docs say env interpolation applies to `apiKey` and headers, not `baseUrl`. I’m checking the loader code to avoid overstating that.

payload.message: One AgentV-specific caveat: when `subprovider` is a custom name, AgentV still passes `--api-key`, but it also strips ambient `OPENAI_*` vars from the Pi CLI child process. I’m checking Pi’s auth priority so the config recommendation won’t depend on a stripped env var.

content.text: One AgentV-specific caveat: when `subprovider` is a custom name, AgentV still passes `--api-key`, but it also strips ambient `OPENAI_*` vars from the Pi CLI child process. I’m checking Pi’s auth priority so the config recommendation won’t depend on a stripped env var.

payload.message:
Yes, we can add a `wtg-openai` custom provider. And yes, your assumption is right: today there is no direct path for a Pi custom provider to read `base_url` from AgentV `targets.yaml`.

Current boundaries:

- Pi custom provider reads `baseUrl` from Pi config/extension.
- AgentV `pi-cli` target `base_url` is only wired for `subprovider: azure`.
- `subprovider: wtg-openai` will make AgentV call `pi --provider wtg-openai`.
- AgentV can pass the key via `--api-key`, but it will not pass a base URL.

Best options:

1. If the WTG endpoint can be committed or generated outside AgentV:
   Add Pi custom provider config, then use:

```yaml
- name: pi
  provider: pi-cli
  subprovider: wtg-openai
  api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
  model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
  grader_target: grader
```

2. If the endpoint must come from env:
   Use a Pi extension/custom provider that reads something like `WTG_OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, not `OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, because AgentV’s `pi-cli` env filtering strips `OPENAI_*` when a custom subprovider is selected.

3. If we want this to be first-class in AgentV:
   Add AgentV core support to synthesize a temporary Pi custom provider from `targets.yaml` when `provider: pi-cli`, `subprovider: <custom>`, and `base_url` are set.

So: custom provider is viable, but `base_url` in `targets.yaml` won’t drive it without an AgentV core change or an upstream Pi `--base-url` feature.

content.text:
Yes, we can add a `wtg-openai` custom provider. And yes, your assumption is right: today there is no direct path for a Pi custom provider to read `base_url` from AgentV `targets.yaml`.

Current boundaries:

- Pi custom provider reads `baseUrl` from Pi config/extension.
- AgentV `pi-cli` target `base_url` is only wired for `subprovider: azure`.
- `subprovider: wtg-openai` will make AgentV call `pi --provider wtg-openai`.
- AgentV can pass the key via `--api-key`, but it will not pass a base URL.

Best options:

1. If the WTG endpoint can be committed or generated outside AgentV:
   Add Pi custom provider config, then use:

```yaml
- name: pi
  provider: pi-cli
  subprovider: wtg-openai
  api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
  model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
  grader_target: grader
```

2. If the endpoint must come from env:
   Use a Pi extension/custom provider that reads something like `WTG_OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, not `OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, because AgentV’s `pi-cli` env filtering strips `OPENAI_*` when a custom subprovider is selected.

3. If we want this to be first-class in AgentV:
   Add AgentV core support to synthesize a temporary Pi custom provider from `targets.yaml` when `provider: pi-cli`, `subprovider: <custom>`, and `base_url` are set.

So: custom provider is viable, but `base_url` in `targets.yaml` won’t drive it without an AgentV core change or an upstream Pi `--base-url` feature.

payload.last_agent_message:
Yes, we can add a `wtg-openai` custom provider. And yes, your assumption is right: today there is no direct path for a Pi custom provider to read `base_url` from AgentV `targets.yaml`.

Current boundaries:

- Pi custom provider reads `baseUrl` from Pi config/extension.
- AgentV `pi-cli` target `base_url` is only wired for `subprovider: azure`.
- `subprovider: wtg-openai` will make AgentV call `pi --provider wtg-openai`.
- AgentV can pass the key via `--api-key`, but it will not pass a base URL.

Best options:

1. If the WTG endpoint can be committed or generated outside AgentV:
   Add Pi custom provider config, then use:

```yaml
- name: pi
  provider: pi-cli
  subprovider: wtg-openai
  api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
  model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
  grader_target: grader
```

2. If the endpoint must come from env:
   Use a Pi extension/custom provider that reads something like `WTG_OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, not `OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, because AgentV’s `pi-cli` env filtering strips `OPENAI_*` when a custom subprovider is selected.

3. If we want this to be first-class in AgentV:
   Add AgentV core support to synthesize a temporary Pi custom provider from `targets.yaml` when `provider: pi-cli`, `subprovider: <custom>`, and `base_url` are set.

So: custom provider is viable, but `base_url` in `targets.yaml` won’t drive it without an AgentV core change or an upstream Pi `--base-url` feature.

```yaml
targets:
  - name: copilot
    provider: copilot
    model: gpt-5-mini
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `model` | No | Model to use (defaults to copilot's default) |
| `cwd` | No | Working directory |
| `subprovider` | No | OpenAI-compatible provider type for `copilot`, `copilot-cli`, or `copilot-sdk`, such as `openai` or `azure` |
| `base_url` | No | Provider base URL or Azure resource URL/name |
| `api_key` | No | Provider API key. Prefer `${{ ENV_VAR }}` references. |
| `bearer_token` | No | Provider bearer token. Prefer `${{ ENV_VAR }}` references. Takes precedence over `api_key` when set. |
| `api_version` | No | Provider API version, primarily for Azure endpoints |
| `wire_api` | No | Provider wire API format, such as `responses` |
| `grader_target` | Yes | LLM target for evaluation |

Route Copilot through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: copilot-openai
    provider: copilot-cli
    subprovider: openai
    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
    wire_api: ${{ COPILOT_PROVIDER_WIRE_API }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

Values can come from environment variables through `${{ ... }}` interpolation. For `copilot-cli`, AgentV maps these flat fields to Copilot's documented provider environment variables before spawning `copilot`; omitted fields leave existing ambient `COPILOT_PROVIDER_*` values unchanged.

## Pi Coding Agent

```yaml
targets:
  - name: pi_target
    provider: pi-coding-agent
    subprovider: openai-codex
    model: gpt-5.5
    thinking: medium
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `subprovider` | No | Pi provider to use, such as `google`, `openai`, `openai-codex`, `azure`, `anthropic`, or `openrouter`. Defaults to Pi's default provider. |
| `model` | No | Model to use. For OpenAI subscription auth through Pi, use `subprovider: openai-codex` with a subscription model such as `gpt-5.5`. |
| `thinking` | No | Pi reasoning level: `off`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, or `xhigh`. Passed to the Pi SDK as `thinkingLevel`. |
| `tools` | No | Comma-separated Pi tool allowlist, such as `read,bash,edit,write`. |
| `api_key` | No | Provider API key. Prefer `${{ ENV_VAR }}` references. Omit for subscription auth handled by Pi. |
| `base_url` | No | Provider base URL or Azure resource URL/name. |
| `cwd` | No | Working directory |
| `timeout_seconds` | No | Per-case timeout |
| `grader_target` | Yes | LLM target for evaluation |

Use `provider: pi-cli` instead when you want AgentV to spawn the `pi` binary directly. It accepts the same Pi fields above plus:

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `executable` | No | Pi binary or shim to run. Defaults to `pi`. |
| `args` | No | Extra arguments appended before the prompt. |

## VS Code

```yaml
targets:
  - name: vscode_dev
    provider: vscode
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `executable` | No | Path to VS Code binary. Supports `${{ ENV_VAR }}` syntax or literal paths. Defaults to `code` (or `code-insiders` for the insiders provider). |
| `grader_target` | Yes | LLM target for evaluation |

*** Begin Patch

 | `base_url` | No | Provider base URL or Azure resource URL/name. |

 | `timeout_seconds` | No | Per-case timeout |
 | `grader_target` | Yes | LLM target for evaluation |
 
+For `provider: pi-coding-agent`, `base_url` is passed through the Pi SDK model
+configuration. This works for OpenAI-compatible endpoints:
+
+```yaml
+targets:
+  - name: pi-sdk-openai
+    provider: pi-coding-agent
+    subprovider: openai
+    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
+    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
+    model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
+    grader_target: azure-base
+```
+
 Use `provider: pi-cli` instead when you want AgentV to spawn the `pi` binary directly. It accepts the same Pi fields above plus:
 
 | Field | Required | Description |
 |-------|----------|-------------|
 | `executable` | No | Pi binary or shim to run. Defaults to `pi`. |
 | `args` | No | Extra arguments appended before the prompt. |
+
+Pi CLI has one important difference from the SDK path: the built-in `openai`
+provider does not currently expose a CLI base-url option. With `provider: pi-cli`
+and `subprovider: openai`, AgentV can pass the API key and model, but `base_url`
+does not re-route the built-in OpenAI provider. For custom endpoints, either
+configure a Pi custom provider in Pi's own `models.json` and reference that
+provider name as `subprovider`, or use Pi's Azure provider path when your gateway
+is compatible with Azure OpenAI Responses:
+
+```yaml
+targets:
+  - name: pi-cli-gateway
+    provider: pi-cli
+    subprovider: azure
+    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
+    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
+    model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
+    grader_target: azure-base
+```
 
 ## VS Code
*** End Patch

The preread block instructs the agent to read input files before processing the query. If a `system_prompt` is configured on the target, it is passed separately via the provider SDK (not in the prompt document).

## Claude

```yaml
targets:
  - name: claude_agent
    provider: claude
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field           | Required | Description                                                                                                                               |
| --------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `executable`    | No       | CLI binary name or path (default: `claude`). Accepts a bare name looked up on PATH (e.g. `claude-zai`) or an absolute/relative file path. |
| `cwd`           | No       | Working directory                                                                                                                         |
| `grader_target` | Yes      | LLM target for evaluation                                                                                                                 |

## cc-mirror

[cc-mirror](https://github.com/numman-ali/cc-mirror) creates isolated Claude Code variants that route through alternative providers (Z.ai, Kimi, MiniMax, OpenRouter, etc.). The `cc-mirror` provider alias resolves to `claude-cli` and auto-discovers the binary path from `~/.cc-mirror/<variant>/variant.json`.

```yaml
targets:
  # Explicit variant with known executable
  - name: claude-zai
    provider: cc-mirror
    executable: claude-zai
    grader_target: azure-base

  # Auto-discover binary from variant.json
  - name: my-kimi
    provider: cc-mirror
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field           | Required | Description                                                                                                                            |
| --------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `executable`    | No       | CLI binary name or path. When set, used directly (skips variant.json lookup).                                                          |
| `variant`       | No       | Variant name (directory under `~/.cc-mirror/`). Defaults to target `name`. Used to locate `variant.json` when `executable` is not set. |
| `cwd`           | No       | Working directory                                                                                                                      |
| `grader_target` | Yes      | LLM target for evaluation                                                                                                              |

Setup a variant first, then reference it by name:

```bash
npx cc-mirror quick --provider zai --name claude-zai --api-key "$Z_AI_API_KEY"
```

Since `cc-mirror` resolves to `claude-cli`, all Claude target fields (model, system_prompt, timeout_seconds, etc.) are also supported.

## Codex CLI

```yaml
targets:
  - name: codex_target
    provider: codex
    executable: codex-eng
    model: ${{ CODEX_MODEL }}
    model_reasoning_effort: ${{ CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field                    | Required | Description                                                                |
| ------------------------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `executable`             | No       | Codex binary or profile shim to run, such as `codex-eng`                   |
| `model`                  | No       | Model to use                                                               |
| `model_reasoning_effort` | No       | Codex SDK reasoning effort: `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, or `xhigh` |
| `cwd`                    | No       | Working directory                                                          |
| `grader_target`          | Yes      | LLM target for evaluation                                                  |

## Copilot CLI

```yaml
targets:
  - name: copilot
    provider: copilot
    model: gpt-5-mini
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field           | Required | Description                                                                                                 |
| --------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `model`         | No       | Model to use (defaults to copilot's default)                                                                |
| `cwd`           | No       | Working directory                                                                                           |
| `subprovider`   | No       | OpenAI-compatible provider type for `copilot`, `copilot-cli`, or `copilot-sdk`, such as `openai` or `azure` |
| `base_url`      | No       | Provider base URL or Azure resource URL/name                                                                |
| `api_key`       | No       | Provider API key. Prefer `${{ ENV_VAR }}` references.                                                       |
| `bearer_token`  | No       | Provider bearer token. Prefer `${{ ENV_VAR }}` references. Takes precedence over `api_key` when set.        |
| `api_version`   | No       | Provider API version, primarily for Azure endpoints                                                         |
| `wire_api`      | No       | Provider wire API format, such as `responses`                                                               |
| `grader_target` | Yes      | LLM target for evaluation                                                                                   |

Route Copilot through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: copilot-openai
    provider: copilot-cli
    subprovider: openai
    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
    wire_api: ${{ COPILOT_PROVIDER_WIRE_API }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

Values can come from environment variables through `${{ ... }}` interpolation. For `copilot-cli`, AgentV maps these flat fields to Copilot's documented provider environment variables before spawning `copilot`; omitted fields leave existing ambient `COPILOT_PROVIDER_*` values unchanged.

## Pi Coding Agent

```yaml
targets:
  - name: pi_target
    provider: pi-coding-agent
    subprovider: openai-codex
    model: gpt-5.5
    thinking: medium
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field             | Required | Description                                                                                                                               |
| ----------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `subprovider`     | No       | Pi provider to use, such as `google`, `openai`, `openai-codex`, `azure`, `anthropic`, or `openrouter`. Defaults to Pi's default provider. |
| `model`           | No       | Model to use. For OpenAI subscription auth through Pi, use `subprovider: openai-codex` with a subscription model such as `gpt-5.5`.       |
| `thinking`        | No       | Pi reasoning level: `off`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, or `xhigh`. Passed to the Pi SDK as `thinkingLevel`.                       |
| `tools`           | No       | Comma-separated Pi tool allowlist, such as `read,bash,edit,write`.                                                                        |
| `api_key`         | No       | Provider API key. Prefer `${{ ENV_VAR }}` references. Omit for subscription auth handled by Pi.                                           |
| `base_url`        | No       | Provider base URL or Azure resource URL/name.                                                                                             |
| `cwd`             | No       | Working directory                                                                                                                         |
| `timeout_seconds` | No       | Per-case timeout                                                                                                                          |
| `grader_target`   | Yes      | LLM target for evaluation                                                                                                                 |

For `provider: pi-coding-agent`, `base_url` is passed through the Pi SDK model
configuration. This works for OpenAI-compatible endpoints:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: pi-sdk-openai
    provider: pi-coding-agent
    subprovider: openai
    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
    model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

Use `provider: pi-cli` instead when you want AgentV to spawn the `pi` binary directly. It accepts the same Pi fields above plus:

| Field        | Required | Description                                 |
| ------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `executable` | No       | Pi binary or shim to run. Defaults to `pi`. |
| `args`       | No       | Extra arguments appended before the prompt. |

Pi CLI has one important difference from the SDK path: the built-in `openai`
provider does not currently expose a CLI base-url option. With `provider: pi-cli`
and `subprovider: openai`, AgentV can pass the API key and model, but `base_url`
does not re-route the built-in OpenAI provider. For custom endpoints, either
configure a Pi custom provider in Pi's own `models.json` and reference that
provider name as `subprovider`, or use Pi's Azure provider path when your gateway
is compatible with Azure OpenAI Responses:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: pi-cli-gateway
    provider: pi-cli
    subprovider: azure
    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
    model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

## VS Code

```yaml
targets:
  - name: vscode_dev
    provider: vscode
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field           | Required | Description                                                                                                                                   |
| --------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `executable`    | No       | Path to VS Code binary. Supports `${{ ENV_VAR }}` syntax or literal paths. Defaults to `code` (or `code-insiders` for the insiders provider). |
| `grader_target` | Yes      | LLM target for evaluation                                                                                                                     |

Using a custom executable path:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: vscode_dev
    provider: vscode
    executable: ${{ VSCODE_CMD }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

## VS Code Insiders

```yaml
targets:
  - name: vscode_insiders
    provider: vscode-insiders
    grader_target: azure-base
```

Same configuration as VS Code.

## Custom CLI Agent

Evaluate any command-line agent:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: local_agent
    provider: cli
    command: "python agent.py --prompt-file {PROMPT_FILE} --output {OUTPUT_FILE}"
    grader_target: azure-base
```

| Field           | Required | Description                                                                                                     |
| --------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `command`       | Yes      | Command to run. `{PROMPT}` is inline prompt text and `{PROMPT_FILE}` is a temp file path containing the prompt. |
| `cwd`           | No       | Working directory                                                                                               |
| `grader_target` | Yes      | LLM target for evaluation                                                                                       |

## Mock Provider

For testing the evaluation harness without calling real providers:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: mock_target
    provider: mock

```

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `subprovider` | No | Pi provider to use, such as `google`, `openai`, `openai-codex`, `azure`, `anthropic`, or `openrouter`. Defaults to Pi's default provider. |
| `model` | No | Model to use. For OpenAI subscription auth through Pi, use `subprovider: openai-codex` with a subscription model such as `gpt-5.5`. |
| `thinking` | No | Pi reasoning level: `off`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, or `xhigh`. Passed to the Pi SDK as `thinkingLevel`. |
| `tools` | No | Comma-separated Pi tool allowlist, such as `read,bash,edit,write`. |
| `api_key` | No | Provider API key. Prefer `${{ ENV_VAR }}` references. Omit for subscription auth handled by Pi. |
| `base_url` | No | Provider base URL or Azure resource URL/name. |
| `cwd` | No | Working directory |
| `timeout_seconds` | No | Per-case timeout |
| `grader_target` | Yes | LLM target for evaluation |

For `provider: pi-coding-agent`, `base_url` is passed through the Pi SDK model
configuration. This works for OpenAI-compatible endpoints:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: pi-sdk-openai
    provider: pi-coding-agent
    subprovider: openai
    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
    model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

Use `provider: pi-cli` instead when you want AgentV to spawn the `pi` binary directly. It accepts the same Pi fields above plus:

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `executable` | No | Pi binary or shim to run. Defaults to `pi`. |
| `args` | No | Extra arguments appended before the prompt. |

Pi CLI has one important difference from the SDK path: the built-in `openai`
provider does not currently expose a CLI base-url option. With `provider: pi-cli`
and `subprovider: openai`, AgentV can pass the API key and model, but `base_url`
does not re-route the built-in OpenAI provider. For custom endpoints, either
configure a Pi custom provider in Pi's own `models.json` and reference that
provider name as `subprovider`, or use Pi's Azure provider path when your gateway
is compatible with Azure OpenAI Responses:

```yaml
targets:
  - name: pi-cli-gateway
    provider: pi-cli
    subprovider: azure
    base_url: ${{ OPENAI_ENDPOINT }}
    api_key: ${{ OPENAI_API_KEY }}
    model: ${{ OPENAI_MODEL }}
    grader_target: azure-base
```

## VS Code
